title: Schema & migrations foundation for S/R signals + expiry calendar
acceptance_criteria: Migration 012 adds to straddle_signals (the params/hypertable-shape table from 003): a NULLABLE sr_subtype TEXT column with a CHECK constraint (e.g. CHECK (sr_subtype IS NULL OR sr_subtype IN ('SR_REVERSAL'))), a NULLABLE sr_strength NUMERIC, a NULLABLE poc_used BOOLEAN, and a NULLABLE level_source JSONB. NO Postgres enum ALTER (reversibility — R-F). Migration is idempotent / safe on re-run and respects the composite PK (id,time) hypertable shape.

Migration 013 creates table index_expiry_calendar (underlying TEXT, expiry_date DATE, is_holiday_shifted BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE, PRIMARY KEY (underlying, expiry_date)) and seeds at minimum NIFTY/BANKNIFTY/SENSEX rows for the current + next ~8 expiries with correct exchange expiry weekdays and any known holiday shifts. Idempotent insert (ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING).

src/db/schema.ts: extend the StraddleSignal interface with sr_subtype, sr_strength, poc_used, level_source (all nullable/optional matching the migration); add a new IndexExpiryCalendar interface matching migration 013.
Both migrations apply cleanly on a fresh DB end-to-end AND on an existing dev DB (migration chain already repaired through 011 — these are 012/013). `bun run --bun tsc --noEmit` is clean.
No write to any signal/engine/router/bootstrap file.
output_format: code plus plain English explanation of every non-obvious decision (esp. expiry weekdays chosen per index and the CHECK-constraint vs enum tradeoff)
